title: Shapes Around Us: A K-2 Vocabulary Pack (English-Spanish)
description: This vocabulary pack introduces basic shapes to K-2 ELL newcomer students, featuring dual-language support, engaging worksheets, and a simple mini-book for hands-on learning.

vocabulary.image_prompt: A simple light blue diamond shape

worksheets.instructions_en: Draw a line from the English word to its Spanish translation and then to the correct shape picture.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Dibuja una línea de la palabra en inglés a su traducción en español y luego a la imagen de la forma correcta.

worksheets.instructions_en: Trace the shape words in English and Spanish. Then draw the shape.
worksheets.instructions_l2: Traza las palabras de las formas en inglés y español. Luego dibuja la forma.

items.image_prompt: Dotted outline of the word 'Circle' and 'Círculo' for tracing, with space to draw a circle
Dotted outline of the word 'Square' and 'Cuadrado' for tracing, with space to draw a square
Dotted outline of the word 'Triangle' and 'Triángulo' for tracing, with space to draw a triangle
Dotted outline of the word 'Rectangle' and 'Rectángulo' for tracing, with space to draw a rectangle
Dotted outline of the word 'Heart' and 'Corazón' for tracing, with space to draw a heart

worksheets.instructions_en: Color the shapes! Look at the words to know what shape it is.
worksheets.instructions_l2: ¡Colorea las formas! Mira las palabras para saber qué forma es.

items.image_prompt: An outline of an oval with 'Oval' and 'Óvalo' written below it.
An outline of a diamond with 'Diamond' and 'Rombo' written below it.
An outline of a hexagon with 'Hexagon' and 'Hexágono' written below it.
An outline of a semicircle with 'Semicircle' and 'Semicírculo' written below it.
An outline of a star with 'Star' and 'Estrella' written below it.

mini_book.title: My Book of Shapes / Mi libro de formas

pages.image_prompt: A title page with various simple colorful shapes and the book title.
A child looking at a collection of different basic shapes.
A scene with everyday objects representing shapes (e.g., a clock for a circle, a window for a square, a slice of pizza for a triangle).

teacher_notes.objective: Students will be able to identify and name basic shapes (circle, square, triangle, rectangle, star, heart, oval, diamond, hexagon, semicircle) in both English and Spanish.
teacher_notes.suggested_use: Introduce shapes using visual aids and real-world examples. Use the mini-book for shared reading and independent practice. Worksheets can be used for reinforcement, assessment, or homework. Encourage students to point out shapes they see in the classroom.
materials_needed: Optional: Real-world objects representing shapes (e.g., a ball, a book, a cracker)
differentiation_tips: **For students needing more support:** Pre-teach vocabulary with flashcards. Provide physical shape manipulatives. Pair with a peer for collaborative activities.
**For students ready for a challenge:** Ask them to draw objects that are specific shapes. Introduce 3D shapes. Have them create a 'shape hunt' in the classroom or at home.

answer_key.answers: Completion of tracing words and drawing shapes.
Completion of coloring the specified shapes.
